appliedUpdates.description: Updated codex-backend.md with force-streaming documentation (readCodexSSEToCompletion, doChatCompletion behavior change, codexHTTPTimeout scope change) and device code flow documentation (constants, endpoints, implementation files)
Marked github-token-discovery.md as DEPRECATED since Copilot no longer uses local token discovery (replaced by device code flow in oauth-refactor)

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The device_code.html template subtitle says 'GitHub Device Code Flow' but is reused for OpenAI Codex flows. Consider adding a note to AGENTS.md about template parameterization conventions for multi-backend templates.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: internal/web/templates/device_code.html:14 subtitle hardcoded to 'GitHub' but used for both Copilot and Codex

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the convention that InitiateLogin/InitiateDeviceCodeLogin return JSON-encoded DeviceCodeLoginInfo as the authURL string, and the web handler detects device code flow vs redirect flow by checking for UserCode in the response. This implicit contract between backend and web layers should be explicit.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both CopilotBackend.InitiateLogin and CodexBackend.InitiateDeviceCodeLogin return JSON as authURL. internal/web/web.go:879-881 detects this pattern.

rejectedObservations.observation: Expired/abandoned device flows accumulate in pending map (copilot device code)
rejectedObservations.reason: acknowledged as minor memory leak, not actionable for library/services/skills

rejectedObservations.observation: pollForAccessToken ignores server-specified interval (copilot device code)
rejectedObservations.reason: non-blocking, GitHub always returns 5s which matches the hardcoded value

rejectedObservations.observation: codexHTTPTimeout comment slightly misleading after force-streaming change
